title: Reducer management — cut on adverse move, re-enter on new signal
acceptance_criteria: ReducerManager implements ManagementHandler interface from T-28
Cut trigger: |current_spot - spot_at_entry| >= personality.params.cut_trigger_points (default 70 points). On trigger: close the position with exit_reason='CUT'
After a CUT, the Reducer personality is in 're-entry eligible' state (in-memory flag per personality, resets at EOD)

Re-entry: when PersonalityRouter delivers the next signal for a Reducer personality in 're-entry eligible' state, the Stage 4 signal quality check uses reentry_min_probability (default 0.65) instead of min_probability (default 0.70). This is handled in PersonalityRouter's fan-out by passing an 'isReentry: boolean' flag in the TradeIntent
Re-entry flag is reset after the re-entry trade is opened, OR at EOD (position monitor midnight reset)

VIX spike re-entry gate: if personality.params.reentry_requires_vix_spike is true (default false for M2), also check that current_vix > entry_vix + reentry_vix_delta before allowing re-entry. Default: disabled
max_daily_trades counts CUT + reentry trades separately — each opening of a new straddle counts as one trade
SL/TSL/TARGET/EOD exits still apply to re-entered positions via evaluateTriggers() — ReducerManager delegates identically to HolderManager for these
Unit tests: cut fires at cut_trigger_points; reentry state set after cut; reentry uses lower min_probability threshold; reentry state reset at EOD; reentry max_daily_trades correctly depleted
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
